authoritative_sources.title: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education, Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Department of Education administers FERPA, and FERPA directly governs student education records, disclosure, consent, and eligible-student rights that constrain event rosters, attendee data, LMS activity, assessment evidence, and event communications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: participant-record retrieval, roster handling, attendance evidence, LMS event activity, disclosure review, and completion evidence packaging

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 46 - Protection of Human Subjects
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Human Research Protections
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OHRP is the federal human-subjects research policy authority, and 45 CFR 46 establishes IRB review, informed consent, and human-subject research requirements that govern research-study events and participant-facing sessions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: research event intake, protocol dependency review, consent-material verification, IRB approval gating, and research compliance escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Clery Act Appendix for FSA Handbook
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education, Federal Student Aid
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal Student Aid guidance is authoritative for Title IV consumer-information obligations, including Clery Act campus safety and security reporting expectations that affect event security assessment, incident preservation, and safety-sensitive routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event security assessment, incident handling during delivery, campus safety routing, and completion hold decisions after safety-sensitive events
used_for: refusal to make safety or reportability findings

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title II Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal ADA enforcement authority, and Title II public-entity rules constrain program access, reasonable modification, and effective communication for public university events and event-related services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessibility checklist review, accommodation request routing, vendor and venue evidence verification, and delivery-ready hold decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Criteria for Accreditation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HLC is a recognized institutional accreditor, and its criteria define expectations for institutional evidence, educational quality, student learning, and cocurricular assessment that shape event evidence without authorizing the event lane to make formal accreditation or assessment findings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cocurricular event evidence packaging, program workflow summary, assessment artifact routing, and accreditation-owner escalation
used_for: event evidence versus formal finding separation

authoritative_sources.title: Major Events Hosted by Non-Departmental Users
authoritative_sources.publisher: University of California, Berkeley Office of Ethics, Risk, and Compliance Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: UC Berkeley's official campus policy is an authoritative public operating reference for a major research university and gives concrete campus event lifecycle checkpoints including student organization registration, venue reservation, security assessment, publicity review, insurance, permits, facility policy, and coordination meetings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event registration, venue hold, security assessment, insurance or permit readiness, coordination meeting, delivery readiness, and owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: Student and Campus Life Event Planning
authoritative_sources.publisher: Cornell University Student and Campus Life
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Cornell's official Student and Campus Life event-planning materials are authoritative for a research university's public event workflow categories, including space reservation, accessible meetings, advisors, contracts, health and safety, youth events, alcohol, food, ticketing, fundraising, facilities support, and event security.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: campus event intake, required form and approval tracking, category-specific prerequisite review, delivery support routing, and closeout evidence mapping

workflow_stages.name: Accepted event intake and source grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm event registration ID, sponsor, organization or department, venue request, date and time, audience, event purpose, tenant policy context, and whether the work is inside campus event execution authority.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and approval evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Validate space reservation, facility use, sponsor approval, registered organization status, security assessment, insurance, permits, accessibility, catering, ticketing, funding, consent, and research or program approval evidence before advancing state.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Participant, learner, and study record handling
workflow_stages.description: Review attendee roster, SIS, LMS, consent, study, accommodation, communication, and assessment artifacts only through authorized retrieval and role-aware disclosure boundaries.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle state progression and exception queue control
workflow_stages.description: Advance, hold, return, rework, or complete event work based on documented execution state, blockers, owners, due dates, and evidence without skipping process steps.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Event delivery monitoring and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Track delivery readiness, active delivery blockers, safety or incident signals, accessibility issues, schedule conflicts, facility constraints, and participant-record conflicts for owner routing.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Create completion or fulfillment records only when delivery, attendance or participation evidence when required, incident check, accessibility follow-up, post-event deliverables, and record locations support closure.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Adjacent authority routing
workflow_stages.description: Route instructional, grading, registrar, accreditation, grants, research ethics, disability accommodation, campus safety, legal, financial approval, or policy exception questions to the correct owner with evidence.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: event registration form, event title, event purpose, requested date and time, event owner, sponsoring department, registered student organization status, student signatory, faculty or staff sponsor, and audience description
venue reservation, venue hold, room assignment, facility use permit, facility policy checklist, event scheduling request, and room-use confirmation
event security assessment, campus safety notes, incident report references, emergency or disruption routing notes, and Clery-sensitive escalation artifacts
accessibility checklist, accommodation request, captioning or interpreter request, accessible seating plan, disability-services owner note, vendor confirmation, and venue configuration record
participant roster, attendance capture, SIS record references, LMS activity references, communication logs, ticketing or registration export, and minimum-necessary disclosure notes
IRB approval record, research administration file, protocol identifier, consent materials, recruitment materials, study-participant communication, and research compliance routing note
program, course, cocurricular, grant, accreditation, assessment, and post-event deliverable evidence with owner and record-location references
insurance, waiver, food, alcohol, minors, fundraising, external speaker, publicity, contract, AV, parking, catering, and facilities support records
execution status update, exception summary, event workflow summary, escalation note, and completion or fulfillment record
systems_of_record: event management or event registration system
campus safety or incident management system
accessibility or disability services case system

allowed: Classify campus event work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, returned, ready, in delivery, complete, or adjacent-owner routed.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete lifecycle state when event registration, schedule, participant, approval, accessibility, safety, consent, delivery, and closeout evidence supports the state.

Identify blockers such as missing sponsor approval, venue conflict, security assessment gap, incomplete accessibility evidence, missing consent, participant-record conflict, or absent completion evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, exception summaries, event workflow summaries, escalation notes, and completion evidence records tied to authoritative records.
Apply retrieved tenant-specific event rules when they are current, source-backed, and consistent with the public baseline and authority map.
escalate: Required student, faculty, program, study, grant, accreditation, accessibility, safety, schedule, consent, or completion records are missing, stale, or materially conflicting.
The request requires instructor, faculty review, registrar, IRB, ethics board, accreditation, grants, disability services, campus safety, legal, financial approval, or policy-override authority.
Public baseline and tenant, jurisdiction, campus, department, facility, grant, IRB, or event policy materially conflict.
A safety, Clery-sensitive, accommodation, minors, alcohol, research, consent, public expression, or learner-record issue changes whether delivery or closeout can proceed.
A stakeholder asks for official evaluation, grading, research ethics approval, accreditation finding, or formal compliance signoff.
refuse: Mark campus event work complete without delivery evidence, attendance or participation evidence when required, incident check, accessibility follow-up, closure owner, and record locations.
Disclose protected learner, faculty, study, participant, accommodation, or assessment records without role authority and documented need.
Approve event policy exceptions, security determinations, facility overrides, accommodation decisions, legal interpretations, budget commitments, or waiver terms.
Impersonate instructor, registrar, faculty reviewer, accreditation reviewer, IRB, ethics-board, disability-services, campus safety, grants, or legal authority.
Decide grades, academic credit, learning-outcome attainment, assessment scores, research protocol acceptability, or participant consent adequacy.

domain_failure_modes: the lane advances a student organization event to approved or delivery-ready while sponsor approval, venue approval, security assessment, insurance, permit, or accessibility evidence is missing
the lane marks an event complete despite absent attendance capture, room-use confirmation, incident check, accessibility follow-up, or post-event deliverable evidence
student roster, LMS activity, attendance, accommodation, or assessment records are disclosed through an unauthorized event workflow
a research recruitment or participant session proceeds without current IRB approval, protocol context, or consent materials and the lane pretends to resolve the ethics question
event attendance evidence is converted into grades, official enrollment certification, learning-outcome signoff, or accreditation findings by the event lane
facility, expressive activity, accessibility, minors, alcohol, external speaker, food, or campus safety conflicts are synthesized into a confident answer without tenant policy precedence
Clery-sensitive, safety, incident, public health, threat, or crowd-control concerns are treated as routine event blockers instead of campus safety or compliance escalations
the industry overlay collapses into generic scheduling and omits higher-education records, consent, academic, research, accreditation, safety, and completion-evidence boundaries

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Scenarios cover missing completion evidence, IRB and consent gaps, FERPA-sensitive roster and LMS records, accessibility evidence gaps, grading and assessment boundaries, and accreditation evidence routing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Program or study workflow blocked on approval
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Scenarios cover student organization sponsor, security, insurance, venue, IRB, disability-services, facility, expressive activity, minors, alcohol, external speaker, and tenant-specific approval blockers.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Scenarios force escalation for IRB or ethics approval, instructor grading, learning-outcome signoff, registrar certification, and accreditation findings.
Queue-level must-pass family covered by missing event approvals, incomplete student-record disclosure authority, and incomplete accessibility readiness evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Queue-level must-pass family covered by research compliance escalation, safety-sensitive event incident routing, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant-specific exception handling.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Queue-level must-pass family covered by refusal to close a symposium without attendance, room-use, incident, accessibility, and post-event evidence.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What student, faculty, or study records govern the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Event execution is governed by event registration, schedule and venue records, sponsor or faculty approval records, SIS and LMS references, participant rosters, attendance records, accommodation logs, research administration and IRB records, consent materials, grant or program files, accreditation or assessment evidence, and post-event closeout artifacts.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What privacy, consent, or assessment boundaries apply?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: FERPA-sensitive learner records require authorized retrieval, minimum-necessary disclosure, and tenant role authority; research events require IRB and consent evidence handled by research compliance or IRB owners; accessibility requests require disability-services ownership; assessment and cocurricular evidence can be packaged but not scored, graded, or represented as accreditation findings by the event lane.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: Where does the lane stop and instructional, grading, or ethics authority begin?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The lane stops at evidence-backed event execution, status, exception, routing, and closeout records. Instructor, faculty review, registrar, IRB, ethics board, accreditation, grants, campus safety, disability-services, legal, and policy owners make formal judgments and approvals.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific event risk thresholds and approval deadlines
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve current campus event policy, venue rules, facility owner requirements, event security thresholds, and department or student organization deadlines before advancing event state.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific public assembly, food, alcohol, minors, and insurance rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Route jurisdictional or legal interpretation to tenant legal, risk, campus safety, or authorized event approver; the lane may only track retrieved requirements and evidence.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a campus event is human-subject research or ordinary program activity
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Use tenant research administration and IRB determinations as record of truth; if absent or conflicting, hold the event and escalate to research compliance or IRB.
